answer: Critical-risk AI systems shall undergo adversarial red-team testing at least quarterly. [Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf]
Security testing results shall be retained for a minimum of seven years. [Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf]

Potential prompt injection incidents shall be escalated to Information Security within one business day (AI-SDL-010, Section 5 – Incident Management, data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf).
Any material change to model architecture, foundation model provider, retrieval corpus, or decision logic shall require re-validation prior to production deployment (AI-SDL-006).

The revised model risk management guidance currently governs model risk management practices [Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf]. This guidance was issued on April 9, 2021 [Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf] and updates the model risk management guidance that was in place since the issuance of SR 11-7 [Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf].

Yes, model risk management activities should be tailored according to model risk. A banking organization may tailor portions of its model risk management practices that are relevant to the risks posed by a particular model [Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf]. Not all models present the same level of risk, and practices should be appropriate and effective for managing the specific risks the banking organization faces or is likely to face [Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf].

The current authoritative model risk management guidance is the Revised Guidance on Model Risk Management (Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf). This revised guidance supersedes and replaces SR letter 11-7, Guidance on Model Risk Management, and SR letter 21-8, Interagency Statement on Model Risk Management for Bank Systems Supporting Bank Secrecy Act/Anti-Money Laundering Compliance (Source: data/documents/current\SR2602.pdf).

Critical-risk AI systems shall obtain approval from the AI Governance Committee before production release [Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf].

Yes, changing the foundation model provider is considered a material change. (Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf, AI-SDL-006)
Yes, changing the retrieval corpus is considered a material change. (Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf, AI-SDL-006)

Yes, minor prompt modifications that do not materially affect system behavior may be approved through expedited review (AI-SDL-007, [Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf]).
Yes, all production AI systems shall maintain immutable audit logs (AI-SDL-008, Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf).

Critical-risk AI systems shall be monitored continuously for security events and model performance degradation (AI-SDL-009, Source: data/documents/current\Synthetic AI Secure Development Lifecycle (AI-SDL) Standard.pdf).
